summary: Backend testing for Web Push notification feature. Created /app/backend/tests/test_push_notifications.py with 15 test cases covering VAPID key endpoint, subscribe/unsubscribe upsert + cross-user isolation, send-test graceful failure, auth gating on all push routes, service-layer 410/404 auto-pruning (with mocked _send_sync), auto-processing hook import smoke, and shared-clip push hook end-to-end. 14/15 PASS. The single failure surfaced a CRITICAL DEAD-CODE BUG in the shared-clip push hook (see backend_issues.critical). Full regression: 94/95 tests pass (was 80/80 before; +15 push tests, -1 failure exposing the dead-code bug). All previously green tests still green — no regressions introduced.

critical.issue: Push hook is DEAD CODE. routes/clips.py is never imported or mounted in server.py — the active handler is server.py:2184 `get_shared_clip_detail` which has NO push notification firing and NO last_view_notify_at throttle. Verified by: (1) grep -n 'from routes.clips\|clips_router' server.py returns ZERO results, (2) hitting /api/shared/clip/{valid_token} returns 200 but last_view_notify_at remains None even after reset, (3) inspecting server.py:2184-2194 confirms the duplicate handler has no push code.

minor.issue: Two handlers defined for the same route — one in routes/clips.py (with push hook + last_view_notify_at throttle) and one in server.py:2184 (without). Even after the dead-code is fixed by mounting clips_router, FastAPI route-resolution conflict will pick whichever is registered first. Recommend deleting the server.py:2184 block and the server.py:2172 `/clips/{clip_id}/share` block, then importing clips_router into the mount loop on server.py:2162.

action_items: Fix dead-code shared-clip push hook: in /app/backend/server.py, (a) add `from routes.clips import router as clips_router` at top and include it in the `for r in (...)` mount loop on line 2162, (b) delete the duplicate `@api_router.post('/clips/{clip_id}/share')` (server.py:2172) and `@api_router.get('/shared/clip/{share_token}')` (server.py:2184) handlers, since equivalents (with the new push hook) already live in routes/clips.py. After fix, re-run the test_shared_clip_route_is_mounted_from_routes_clips test to confirm last_view_notify_at gets populated and 2nd hit within 6h does not advance the marker.

Optional polish (services/push_notifications.py): the 24h TTL on webpush is hardcoded; consider env override. Also _send_sync swallows generic Exception with str(e)[:100] — could log the full exception for diagnosis.
critical_code_review_comments: [CRITICAL DEAD CODE] routes/clips.py defines GET /shared/clip/{share_token}, POST /clips/{clip_id}/share, GET /shared/clip/{share_token}/video plus other handlers, but `clips_router` is never `include_router`'d in server.py. The new push notification hook (routes/clips.py:97-116) is therefore unreachable. The active route is server.py:2184 (`get_shared_clip_detail`), a stale duplicate that doesn't fire the push. This was not caught during self-verification because the route returns 200 either way — only the side-effect (push send + last_view_notify_at update) is missing.

[Architectural] server.py is 2347 lines and contains route handlers that overlap with dedicated routers in routes/. Recommend a one-time cleanup pass to delete all `@api_router.*` clip/share/folder handlers from server.py and rely solely on the per-feature routers, to prevent silent dead-code regressions like this one.

services/push_notifications.py:_send_sync uses `getattr(e.response, 'status_code', 0)` which can return 0 if pywebpush raises a non-HTTP error — pruning logic in send_to_user only matches '410' or '404' substrings, so transient network errors will never be pruned (correct behavior, but log volume could grow). Consider a separate counter for permanent vs transient failures.

routes/push_notifications.py: `unsubscribe` accepts `input: dict` (no Pydantic model) — works but inconsistent with `subscribe` which uses SubscribeInput. Add an UnsubscribeInput Pydantic model for parity and OpenAPI clarity.
routes/push_notifications.py: VAPID_PUBLIC_KEY is read at module import time (line 13). If env var changes after worker spin-up the cached value is stale — fine for current usage, just call it out.

success_rate.backend: 93% — 14/15 new push tests pass + 80/80 prior regression = 94/95 total. The 1 failure (test_shared_clip_route_is_mounted_from_routes_clips) is correctly catching a real dead-code bug in the shared-clip push hook integration; it should be retained as a regression guard until fixed.

test_credentials: testcoach@demo.com / password123 (loaded from /app/memory/test_credentials.md). Cross-user isolation test creates a throwaway test_push_<uuid>@demo.com user via /api/auth/register on each run.
seed_data_creation: test_push_<uuid>@demo.com user created during cross-user isolation test (not deleted — accumulates 1 user per run; harmless but note for cleanup). Plus one fake push subscription doc keyed on a non-routable FCM-style endpoint, created and deleted within test_send_to_user_prunes_410_and_increments_removed (cleaned up on teardown). No persistent dirty data left after a successful run.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Push feature is mostly green. The single CRITICAL issue is that routes/clips.py is not registered with the FastAPI app — fix is straightforward (add 1 import + 1 line in mount loop in server.py, delete 2 duplicate route blocks). After the fix, re-run only `pytest /app/backend/tests/test_push_notifications.py::TestHookIntegration::test_shared_clip_route_is_mounted_from_routes_clips -v` to confirm. The auto-processing hook (server.py:1201-1211) was verified by code inspection — it lives directly in server.py inside run_auto_processing, imports send_to_user inline, wraps in try/except so push failures never break the AI pipeline. Could not exercise it end-to-end without uploading a video and waiting ~10min for full pipeline; recommend adding an integration test that mocks the AI calls and asserts send_to_user is invoked exactly once with title='Match analysis ready'. The 410-pruning is fully unit-tested via monkeypatching services.push_notifications._send_sync.
rca of the issue:
RCA — Shared-clip push hook is dead code:
  Reproduction: 1) reset last_view_notify_at=None on any clip with a share_token, 2) GET /api/shared/clip/{token}, 3) read last_view_notify_at — expected ISO timestamp, actual None.
  Root cause: server.py never registers clips_router. grep confirms zero references to `routes.clips` or `clips_router` in server.py. The active handler is server.py:2184 (get_shared_clip_detail), a pre-existing duplicate that lacks the new push code added to routes/clips.py.
  Mitigation: 2-line fix in server.py — add `from routes.clips import router as clips_router` and include it in the for-loop on line 2162. Then delete the 2 duplicate handlers in server.py (lines 2172-2194) to eliminate route ambiguity.
  Severity: HIGH — feature requirement '6-hour throttle on shared-clip-viewed push' is non-functional in production. Coaches will never receive notifications when their shared clips are opened, despite the UI Enable Push toggle working correctly.
